compiled_state.title: As a developer, I can validate the complete worktree lifecycle through E2E tests
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Description

**What**: E2E test exercising the complete worktree development lifecycle from creation through merge
**Why**: The single largest bug category (34 fixes/month) involves multi-script workflow interactions that only manifest when the full chain executes in sequence
**Scope**:
- IN: Worktree creation, ticket system init, ticket create/transition/compact/comment, commit with hooks, merge-to-main, version bump, main state verification
- OUT: Multi-worktree scenarios (Story 4), cross-cutting compact scenarios (Story 3)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, an E2E test creates a worktree, initializes the ticket system, creates and transitions a ticket, commits with hooks (DSO_E2E_MODE=1), runs merge-to-main, and verifies git log on main shows the expected merge commit
  ← Satisfies: "E2E tests exercise the complete worktree lifecycle"
- When this story is complete, the test explicitly configures version.file_path in dso-config.conf and creates a version file, then verifies the version was incremented on main after merge
  ← Satisfies: "verify main state (git log, ticket state, version file)"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] DSO_E2E_MODE bypass must align with Story 1's implementation — commit messages need valid ticket ID format for non-bypassed hooks
- [Performance] Full lifecycle involves multiple git commits — test execution time should be monitored
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
